title: Marine Superintendent (HSEQ / Operations)
description:
Marine Operations Oversight

  • Oversee day-to-day marine operations of assigned vessels, ensuring safe navigation,
  • cargo operations, and adherence to company procedures
  • Provide operational guidance and decision support to Masters and vessel crews
  • Review and support voyage planning, cargo operations, and marine fuel transfer activities

HSEQ Management and Compliance

  • Implement and maintain the Safety Management System in accordance with the ISM Code
  • Ensure compliance with MPA requirements, flag state regulations, class rules, and international conventions including SOLAS, MARPOL, and STCW
  • Support safe handling, storage, and transfer of alternative fuels including biofuels and methanol in accordance with approved procedures and risk assessments

Audits, Inspections, and Assurance

  • Conduct vessel inspections, internal audits, and operational assessments
  • Prepare vessels for inspections and audits by MPA, flag state, class, charterers, and terminal operators
  • Track, monitor, and close out audit findings, incidents, near misses, and corrective actions

Incident Management and Risk Control

  • Lead or support investigations into marine incidents, non-conformities, and near misses
  • Identify operational risks and implement preventive and corrective measures
  • Support emergency preparedness, response planning, and drills Operational and Stakeholder Coordination
  • Work closely with Chartering, Post-Fixture, Technical, and Commercial teams to support safe and efficient voyage execution
  • Liaise with terminals, pilots, agents, surveyors, and service providers on marine safety and operational matters
  • Support customer and counterparty marine assurance requirements

Training and Safety Culture

  • Support crew competency, familiarisation, and training related to HSEQ, cargo operations, and alternative fuels
  • Promote and reinforce a strong safety culture across shipboard and shore-based teams

Qualifications

  • Master Mariner (Foreign Going), or
  • Chief Mate with relevant tanker endorsement
    Sailing Experience

Sailing experience on one or more of the following vessel types is required:

  • Chemical tankers
  • Product tankers
  • LNG carriers

Additional Requirements

  • Shore-based experience in marine operations, HSEQ, or superintendent roles is preferred
  • Strong working knowledge of ISM, ISPS, MARPOL, and MPA regulatory frameworks
  • Familiarity with alternative fuels such as biofuels and methanol is an advantage
  • Ability to work independently with sound judgement in a safety-critical environment
  • Strong communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills

districts.location: D23 Hillview, Dairy Farm, Bukit Panjang, Choa Chu Kang
